title: hypolepis rugosula
intro: Hypolepis rugosula, also known as the rough-leaved maidenhair fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Dicksoniaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Hypolepis rugosula include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and anticancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
